tasks.title: Author the fact-checker AGENT (verification engine) + extract-claims.py
tasks.description: Create the verification ENGINE as a subagent at claude-code-shared/agents/fact-checker.md, modeled on the existing agent format (see agents/shared-test-agent.md): frontmatter with name, description, tools, model — and a body that is a system-prompt persona, NOT procedural skill steps. The original copied-from-the-internet file IS already an agent ('You are a Fact-Checker... Use PROACTIVELY', tools: Read/Write/Edit/WebSearch/WebFetch); salvage its good structure but strip ALL embedded Python pseudocode (it calls unimplemented self._ methods). The agent runs in an isolated context window so its many web searches do not pollute the main thread; it returns a compact final report to the caller. Agent behavior: (1) Extract verifiable claims only — statistical, attribution, causal, temporal, comparative. Skip opinions, design decisions, vague assertions. Run the deterministic pre-pass script (see below) first, then catch anything it missed. (2) Verify every claim with mandatory live web search — never reasoning-only, since the model cannot catch its own hallucinations by reasoning. (3) Every verdict that is not a clean TRUE must ship a citable, openable source link so the user can confirm the discrepancy independently. (4) Verdict uses the 6-tier scale: TRUE / MOSTLY_TRUE / PARTLY_TRUE / MOSTLY_FALSE / FALSE / UNVERIFIABLE. (5) Output is always advisory — flag findings with severity + citations, never block. (6) Return the report in a structured form grouped by verdict severity so the calling skill can render inline or write to a file. Agent sets its own model in frontmatter directly (agents are NOT tiered via skill-tiers.json) — choose a capable model (sonnet) since verdict judgment matters; tools must include WebSearch and WebFetch. ALSO build the one genuinely deterministic piece as a real script: claude-code-shared/skills/fact-check/scripts/extract-claims.py (regex claim-detection salvaged from the original extract_verifiable_claims). It reads text from stdin or a file arg and emits candidate claims grouped by the 5 types as JSON. The agent invokes this script by absolute path as a cheap pre-pass.
acceptance_criteria: agents/fact-checker.md exists with agent frontmatter (name, description, tools, model) matching the shared-test-agent format
Agent body is a system-prompt persona, not numbered skill steps; no embedded Python pseudocode or self._ references remain
tools list includes WebSearch and WebFetch; model is set explicitly (sonnet)
Agent mandates live web search per claim and forbids reasoning-only verification
Agent documents the 5 extractable claim types and that opinions/design decisions are skipped
Agent requires any non-TRUE verdict to include a citable, openable source link
Agent documents the full 6-tier verdict scale and returns findings grouped by verdict severity
Agent output is advisory/flag-only, never blocking
scripts/extract-claims.py exists, is executable, reads stdin or a file arg, and emits JSON grouped by the 5 claim types
extract-claims.py runs cleanly on a sample tweet and a sample PRD paragraph and returns sensible candidates
Agent instructs running extract-claims.py by absolute path as a deterministic pre-pass before web verification

tasks.title: Build the thin /fact-check SKILL that spawns the fact-checker agent
tasks.description: Rewrite claude-code-shared/skills/fact-check/SKILL.md as a THIN manual entry point matching the established skill convention (see debug, code-review, tasks-to-linear). It does NOT contain verification logic — that lives in the fact-checker agent from T-0001. Skill responsibilities: (1) Invoked manually via /fact-check against any pasted input, from a single tweet to a full PRD. (2) Spawn the fact-checker agent (Agent tool, subagent_type fact-checker) passing the input, so the token-heavy web search runs in isolation and only the report returns. (3) Handle ADAPTIVE output: for small inputs (single claim / short content) render the agent's report inline in chat; for document-scale inputs with many claims, write the report to docs/fact-checks/ grouped by verdict severity, then summarize inline with the file path. (4) Relay findings as advisory only — never block. Frontmatter must include name, description, model, effort (model/effort stamped by T-0004 sync; placeholder values fine). Use numbered procedural steps. Keep it thin: capture input, spawn agent, route output. Document that the same fact-checker agent is what future inline skills (to-prd-html, to-tasks, run-tasks) will spawn at the end of their runs, so logic is never duplicated.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md is thin: captures input, spawns the fact-checker agent, routes output — contains no claim-extraction or verification logic itself
Skill spawns the fact-checker agent via the Agent tool (subagent_type fact-checker)
Skill implements adaptive output: inline for small inputs, file at docs/fact-checks/ (grouped by severity) for document-scale, with an inline summary + path
Skill relays findings as advisory/flag-only and never blocks
Skill notes the same agent is the reuse point for future inline embedding in other skills
Frontmatter has name, description, model, effort (model/effort may be placeholders pending T-0004 sync)

tasks.title: Add placeholder for the inline code-currency fact-check (agent + future skill)
tasks.description: Create placeholders for the FUTURE inline code-currency fact-checker, following the same agent-engine pattern as fact-check. Intentionally stubs — reserve names and record grill-session design intent for a later dedicated grill session. Create: (a) a code-currency AGENT stub at claude-code-shared/agents/code-currency-checker.md (agent frontmatter, marked PLACEHOLDER / NOT YET IMPLEMENTED), and optionally (b) a note in the fact-check skill or a sibling skill stub describing where it embeds. Record the scoped intent: this engine verifies CODE CURRENCY not factual truth — e.g. 'are we on the latest TanStack version', 'are these modern Django patterns'. Source strategy is Context7 MCP docs first, web search second (NOT raw web first). Verdict semantics are current-vs-outdated, not true-vs-false. It runs at the END of a host workflow (to-prd-html, to-tasks, run-tasks) and APPENDS a verification summary rather than pausing mid-execution. Findings advisory/flag-only. Note it needs its own grill session before build. Agent sets its own model in frontmatter (not tiered).
acceptance_criteria: agents/code-currency-checker.md stub exists with agent frontmatter, marked PLACEHOLDER / NOT YET IMPLEMENTED at the top
Stub records: code-currency scope, Context7-first then web source strategy, current-vs-outdated verdict semantics, append-at-end host-workflow behavior, advisory-only output
Stub notes it requires its own dedicated grill session before implementation
Stub frontmatter follows the agent convention (name, description, tools, model)

tasks.title: Register the /fact-check skill in tiering so hooks don't miss it
tasks.description: Register the thin /fact-check SKILL from T-0002 in the central tier map so tier-advisor.sh nudges it and cc-usage-benchmark.py includes it in weekly adherence. NOTE the agent/skill split: AGENTS (fact-checker, code-currency-checker) are NOT registered in skill-tiers.json — they self-declare model in their own frontmatter and are not tracked by the tiering/usage infra. Only the SKILL needs registration. Both hooks read claude-code-shared/resources/skill-tiers.json — an unregistered skill gets no tier advisory AND is silently missing from the usage report. Steps: (1) Add 'fact-check' to the 'skills' map in skill-tiers.json. Assign T2 (mechanical, well-defined scope — the skill is a thin spawn+route shim; the heavy reasoning lives in the agent which is untiered). (2) Do NOT add fact-check to the 'delegate' array — it does not itself do heavy lookups; it spawns the agent which does. (3) Run scripts/sync-skill-tiers.py --check to preview, then --apply to stamp model+effort into the skill frontmatter. (4) Confirm tier-advisor resolves a tier for '/fact-check'.
acceptance_criteria: skill-tiers.json 'skills' map contains 'fact-check' set to T2
fact-check is NOT added to the 'delegate' array
Agents (fact-checker, code-currency-checker) are NOT added to skill-tiers.json
sync-skill-tiers.py --apply runs without error and stamps model:+effort: into the fact-check skill frontmatter
tier-advisor logic against '/fact-check' resolves a non-empty tier (T2) and emits the advisory block
git diff shows skill-tiers.json and the fact-check SKILL.md frontmatter updated consistently, no unrelated changes

tasks.title: Build a register-skill meta-skill covering both skills and agents
tasks.description: Create a new skill (claude-code-shared/skills/register-skill/SKILL.md) that codifies the registration checklist for BOTH skills and agents, so new additions never miss the infra wiring again. It must distinguish the two paths. SKILL path: both hooks read claude-code-shared/resources/skill-tiers.json — tier-advisor.sh looks up the skill there to nudge model/effort (unregistered = no nudge), and cc-usage-benchmark.py builds the expected model from cfg['skills'] (unregistered = missing from weekly adherence). sync-skill-tiers.py --apply stamps model:/effort: into each skill's frontmatter from that map and also runs via the pre-commit hook. AGENT path: agents live in claude-code-shared/agents/, set their own model: in frontmatter, are NOT in skill-tiers.json, and are NOT tracked by tier-advisor or the usage benchmark — so registering an agent means correct frontmatter (name/description/tools/model) and a valid agents-dir file, nothing in the tier map. The skill should be invokable (/register-skill <name>) and: (1) Detect whether the target is a skill or an agent (which dir it lives in). (2) For a skill: recommend a tier (explain T1-T4 from skill-tiers.json), ask the user to confirm, ask whether it belongs in the 'delegate' array, edit skill-tiers.json, run sync --check then --apply, verify the name resolves a tier and will be counted by cc-usage-benchmark.py's SKILL_BODY_RE. (3) For an agent: verify frontmatter has name/description/tools/model and the file is in the agents dir; confirm NO skill-tiers.json entry is needed. (4) Report a summary of what was wired. Match repo skill convention (name/description/model/effort frontmatter, numbered steps, delegate read-only lookups to caveman:cavecrew-investigator). Register THIS skill itself (T2). Document the full registration chain inline as the single canonical reference.
acceptance_criteria: register-skill/SKILL.md exists with name/description/model/effort frontmatter
Skill documents BOTH registration paths: skill (tiered via skill-tiers.json + sync) and agent (self-declared model, not tiered, not in usage benchmark)
Skill detects whether the target is a skill or agent and branches accordingly
Skill path: recommends tier, edits skill-tiers.json, runs sync --check then --apply, verifies tier resolution and usage-report counting
Agent path: verifies agent frontmatter + dir placement and explicitly confirms no skill-tiers.json entry is needed
register-skill is itself registered in skill-tiers.json (T2) and its frontmatter is stamped by sync --apply
Skill delegates read-only lookups to caveman:cavecrew-investigator per global CLAUDE.md

follow_ups.title: Schedule a dedicated grill session for the inline code-currency engine
steps: After /fact-check is in use and lessons learned are gathered, run /grill-me against the code-currency-checker agent placeholder from T-0003
Resolve its open design branches: exact extraction of code/version claims, Context7-vs-web source ordering, current-vs-outdated verdict thresholds, and which host skills spawn it and where
Convert the resolved design into its own task file via /to-tasks or a manual tasks JSON

follow_ups.title: Verify the weekly usage report counts /fact-check after first real use
steps: After /fact-check has been invoked at least once in a real session, run the cc-usage-analytics weekly report (cc-usage-benchmark.py)
Confirm 'fact-check' appears in the per-skill tier-adherence section and is matched against T2 (sonnet)
If missing, check the skills/<name>/SKILL.md path token against SKILL_BODY_RE in cc-usage-benchmark.py and that the name in skill-tiers.json matches exactly
